description: 90/10 budget split on file_backup and database_migration (H260 redo)

experiments.notes: checkpoint_4 timed out during agent but has eval results
checkpoint_3 timed out; only 3/5 checkpoints completed
Only 1/4 checkpoints completed; 90/10 split cost too high per checkpoint
Only 1/5 checkpoints completed; 90/10 split cost high per checkpoint

findings.summary: The 90/10 budget split with two-agent approach is significantly more expensive per checkpoint than single-agent, limiting coverage. file_backup two-agent had very low pass rate (12.5%) at 2.7x the single-agent cost. database_migration two-agent matched single-agent pass rate on checkpoint_1 but at 11x the cost. Results are inconclusive for H260 due to insufficient checkpoint coverage from budget constraints.
findings.budget_mismatch: The two-agent runner allocates the full budget as cost_limit per slop_code invocation rather than dividing across checkpoints, causing each checkpoint to potentially consume the entire budget.
